name: debug-map-blast-radius
title: Failure Surface Map
description: Map a concrete failure into reproduction scope, impacted paths, and observed-versus-expected boundaries before deeper debugging. Use when a bug exists but the failing surface is still diffuse.
purpose: Reduce a broad bug report to a bounded reproduction window and impacted code surface before root-cause work.
when_to_use: A failure is reported but the affected modules or boundaries are still unclear.
Need to map observed versus expected behavior before debugging deeper.
Need a reproducible scope window for a bug hunt.
do_not_use: Need the final code fix directly.
Need general repository analysis without a concrete failure symptom.
Already have a confirmed root cause.

required_inputs.name: TARGET_SCOPE
required_inputs.description: Scope in which the failure appears.

required_inputs.name: EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR
required_inputs.description: Expected behavior instead of the failure.

required_inputs.name: REPRO_HINTS
required_inputs.description: Known triggering inputs, timing, or environment hints.

structured_outputs.name: REPRO_WINDOW
structured_outputs.description: Minimal reproduction scope and trigger conditions.

structured_outputs.name: IMPACTED_PATHS
structured_outputs.description: Files, modules, or boundaries most likely involved.

structured_outputs.name: NEXT_DEBUG_ENTRY_POINTS
structured_outputs.description: Cheapest next entry points for debugging.

neutrality_rules: Map the failing surface without asserting a root cause.
Keep suspected modules tentative until evidence confirms them.
If reproduction remains unclear, state the smallest missing condition explicitly.
default_program: [stages: preflight>detect>analyze>review>handoff>audit | scope: repo|diff|paths(glob,...) | policy: evidence,deterministic-output | lens: feynman | output: md(contract=v1)]
procedure: Normalize the failure symptom and expected behavior into one comparison table.
Derive the smallest reproduction window from the given evidence.
Identify the most likely impacted paths and system boundaries.
Return the cheapest next debugging entry points without claiming root cause.
mandatory_rules: Do not jump directly to the fix.
Keep the output focused on scope reduction and entry-point selection.
example_invocation:
$debug-map-blast-radius
TARGET_SCOPE: src/auth
FAILURE_SYMPTOM: login succeeds but session is lost after refresh
EXPECTED_BEHAVIOR: session persists after refresh
lens_rationale: Failure mapping should shrink the problem space using observable repro evidence.
